record.field: Activepieces is an open-source, no-code automation platform that lets teams build AI-powered workflows without per-task fees or vendor lock-in.

Activepieces is an open-source, no-code automation platform that lets teams build AI-powered workflows without per-task fees or vendor lock-in.

It connects apps, APIs and AI models through a visual flow builder, serving as a self-hostable alternative to Zapier, Make and Tray. Integrations are written as type-safe TypeScript "pieces" that the community can extend, and any piece automatically doubles as a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server usable from LLM clients like Claude, Cursor and Windsurf. It targets both non-technical builders who want a simple drag-and-drop experience and engineering teams that need to keep automation and data on their own infrastructure.

Key features
  • Visual no-code flow builder with triggers, branches, loops and auto-retries
  • Hundreds of native integration "pieces" plus inline HTTP requests and custom code steps
  • Native AI actions and MCP servers to wire workflows into LLM agents
  • Human-in-the-loop steps: approval gates, forms and chat interfaces
  • Type-safe TypeScript framework for building custom pieces with hot reloading
  • Self-hosted and network-gapped deployment via Docker for full data control
Best for

Teams that want to automate business processes and AI agent workflows on their own infrastructure, avoid per-run pricing, and extend integrations in code while keeping sensitive data on-premise.

services: activepieces: image: activepieces/activepieces:0.35.0 restart: unless-stopped depends_on: postgres: condition: service_healthy redis: condition: service_healthy environment: AP_ENGINE_EXECUTABLE_PATH: dist/packages/engine/main.js AP_API_KEY: ${AP_API_KEY} AP_ENCRYPTION_KEY: ${AP_ENCRYPTION_KEY} AP_JWT_SECRET: ${AP_JWT_SECRET} AP_ENVIRONMENT: prod AP_FRONTEND_URL: https://{{ instance.full_domain }} AP_WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS: 30 AP_TRIGGER_DEFAULT_POLL_INTERVAL: 5 AP_POSTGRES_DATABASE: activepieces AP_POSTGRES_HOST: postgres AP_POSTGRES_PORT: 5432 AP_POSTGRES_USERNAME: activepieces AP_POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${AP_POSTGRES_PASSWORD} AP_EXECUTION_MODE: UNSANDBOXED AP_REDIS_HOST: redis AP_REDIS_PORT: 6379 AP_SANDBOX_RUN_TIME_SECONDS: 600 AP_TELEMETRY_ENABLED: 'false' AP_TEMPLATES_SOURCE_URL: https://cloud.activepieces.com/api/v1/flow-templates container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:80' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network postgres: image: postgres:14 restart: unless-stopped environment: POSTGRES_DB: activepieces POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${AP_POSTGRES_PASSWORD} POSTGRES_USER: activepieces volumes: - ./postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U activepieces -d activepieces interval: 30s timeout: 30s retries: 3 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' networks: - internal redis: image: redis:7 restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./redis_data:/data healthcheck: test: - CMD - redis-cli - ping interval: 30s timeout: 30s retries: 3 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_redis' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
